entry.title: 利用 SAP 的企业自动化处理雇用到退休流程
entry.content:
本文为翻译文，原文链接Leverage Enterprise Automation with SAP for Hire-to-Retire processes，原文作者Manuel_Namyslo。

简介

在不断变化的劳动力管理环境中，“从雇佣到退休”的概念已成为一种超越传统招聘流程的变革性方法。雇用到退休包含全面的战略框架，强调员工在组织内的整个旅程，从初始雇用阶段到最终退休。还可以按以下步骤进行汇总：

从雇佣到退休的理念不仅仅是一个人力资源流行词；这是一种整体的人才管理方法，它凸显了员工职业生涯的每个阶段在组织中的重要性。通过专注于招聘、持续学习、绩效管理、员工幸福感和无缝的退休过渡，企业能够打造一支灵活且富有韧性的员工队伍，在不断变化的业务环境中实现持续成功。为了确保这一持续成功，每个组织都需要提供顺畅的人力资源流程，以避免常见的难题：

 

  • 招聘流程效率低下
  • 入职体验不佳
  • 缺少持续学习机会
  • 绩效管理效率低下
  • 员工敬业度有限
  • 继任计划不足
  • 忽视员工幸福感
  • 退休过渡面临的挑战
  • 技术和数据管理不足

 

在本博文中，我想向您展示如何利用企业自动化来克服雇用到退休领域的低效流程、重复性任务和不良的流程管理。但首先，什么是 SAP 的企业自动化？

 

什么是企业自动化？

借助 SAP 的企业自动化功能，我们的客户能够利用 SAP Integration Suite、SAP Signavio 和 SAP Build 等一流的工具，快速、轻松地洞察业务流程，并利用合适的工具实现自动化，实现出色的业务成果。这些工具与现有业务应用程序无缝协作，不会增加复杂性：

 

  • SAP Integration Suite 是一款集成平台即服务 (iPaaS)，具有数千个与 SAP 和非 SAP 应用程序的预构建集成、API 和连接器。它降低了企业预置和多云环境的复杂性，可帮助您实现优化的端到端业务流程。

 

  • SAP Signavio 提供基于 200,000 个行业基准和最佳实践的业务流程可见性、潜在瓶颈、根本原因分析以及改进建议。深入了解效率低下有助于组织优化和自动化流程。

 

  • SAP Build 是一款低代码解决方案，可以轻松拖放创建自动化和应用。它包括面向 SAP 和非 SAP 系统的预构建自动化和连接器。业务专家可以使用 SAP Build 快速解决流程低效问题。

 

 

雇用到退休的预构建内容：

从 SAP 的企业自动化开始的最简单方法是使用预构建内容。预构建内容提供技术部件，您可以实施这些部件，以及直接来自 SAP 开发团队和合作伙伴的最佳实践和建议。您可以在此处找到我们的内容：

SAP Build – 预构建内容

在此，您可以找到一些专为优化雇用到退休而设计的内容示例：

录用通知书分发 – SuccessFactors

SAP ECC 监控和健康检查

授权推出机器人

职位需求的创建和审批

创建社会保障证书

合同员工结束日期扩展

筛选问题 (API) - SuccessFactors

EC 工作流自动化 – SuccessFactors

面向 SAP SuccessFactors 中电子分离的流程自动化包

重复简历标识的流程自动化

生成离职员工清单（使用 SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central）(4P4)

列出离职员工的未完成活动 (4JF)

工作录用通知审批

 

将此内容转化成您的优势，因为它不仅为您提供可在 SAP Build Lobby 中部署的技术部件，还为您提供来自开发团队和合作伙伴且经认证可上载 SAP Build 内容的建议和最佳实践。此外，我们还确保定期更新和维护内容。

 

用例

 

下一步，我们要跳转到一个实用的方案，向您展示如何将企业自动化用于雇用到退休。在任何企业中，入职流程都是一项至关重要的活动，会影响新员工的体验和成功。我个人是 sap 的工作学生，每隔 6 个月轮流到新部门。因此，我经常面对入职流程，需要新的培训和系统访问权限来支持团队。通过这种体验，我可以说，如果不正确，入职流程可能涉及手动和重复性任务，从而导致低效和延迟。因此，企业需要一款能够实现无缝集成、流程自动化和定制的解决方案，为新员工提供个性化的入职体验。


SAP Integration Suite 与 SAP Build Process Automation 和 SAP Signavio 相结合，提供了全面的解决方案来解决入职流程中的挑战。通过利用基于事件的集成模式、低代码/无代码工作流以及 Business Technology Platform 的强大功能，企业能够扩展和定制登录体验，与第三方工具集成，自动化活动并简化共享服务流程。

 

作为第一步，我们建议使用 SAP Signavio Process Insights 评估新员工的内部入职流程，并确定低效的流程步骤。

瓶颈和手动任务会导致新员工和 HR 业务伙伴的用户体验不满意。

 

在 SAP Signavio Process Intelligence 中，您可以执行根本原因分析以分析入职流程中容易出错且可能导致较长周期的各种步骤。

其中包括为新员工提供合适的设备、分配必要的培训以及负责系统对各种软件组件的访问。

 

利用 SAP Integration Suite 利用基于事件的通信主动启动流程自动化。

您可以启用应用程序、集成和扩展以通过业务事件异步通信 - 在本例中是 SAP SuccessFactors。

我们可以订阅业务事件，以便在新员工进入系统后收到通知。在消息传递代理中，您可以查看租用来自 SAP SuccessFactors 的业务事件的队列。现在，每次生成新录用员工时，都会注意到这一点，并触发了预定义的自动活动。 

 

在下一步中，我们将根据业务规则和决策表自动触发新流程，以识别正确的培训分配和设备。为此，我们可以利用 SAP Build Process Automation 的 LCNC 设计时间。

我们可以通过消息传递代理与来自 SAP SuccessFactors 的上下文数据进行交互。 

 

 

通过嵌入式决策功能，您可以将业务逻辑应用于流程。 

在此，它用于确定根据工作角色适用的培训和设备。 可以轻松地对此进行定制，例如添加附加参数或数学运算符。

 

HR 业务伙伴在 我的收件箱 应用程序中接收此任务，并且可以在一个位置审核所有相关数据。他们可以发表评论并批准或拒绝表单，以便进行人工干预。 

批准此流程后，我们可以将数据处理回 SAP SuccessFactors。

 

我们还可以利用自定义集成流连接到第三方应用程序，并将适配器用于多个通信协议。

在此，我们在 SAP Ariba 中生成采购申请，以确保正确的设备在工作第一天可用。

 

因此，在 SAP Ariba 中生成采购申请，并且已在 SAP Service Cloud 中为 IT 管理员维护 IT 消息。

 

结论

 

Enterprise Automation 目前是 SAP 的最热门主题，它允许您在异构环境中集成应用程序、发现低效环节和自动化流程。投资这项计划必将带来长期的业务成果，并采用完善的方法自动运行和管理企业内部的自动化流程。

这只是如何在雇用到退休上下文中使用这些技术的示例。请记住，此框架具有高度适应性，可用于任何业务范围、行业或用例。因此，请务必查看我们的其他资料、赋能和文档，开启 SAP 企业自动化之旅：

 

  • 请访问 sap.com/enterprise-automation
  • 查看 SAP Build Store 中的预构建集成和自动化内容
  • 查看《教程导航器》中的“实践”材料和说明

 

当然，如果你想快速开启低代码开发之旅，加入我们的 SAP Builders 社区，开始学习 SAP Build 解决方案！

entry.title: 利用SAP 企业自动化处理寻源到付款流程
entry.content:
本文为翻译文，原文链接Leverage Enterprise Automation with SAP for Source-to-Pay processes，原文作者Manuel_Namyslo。

 

简介

寻源到付款世界是全球企业采购和财务管理领域的基石。从确定采购需求到最终向供应商付款这一全面的流程，在优化效率、降低成本和确保合规性方面发挥着关键作用。寻源到付款流程的重要性是多方面的，包括完成以下操作的多个步骤：

 

最后，寻源到付款流程是现代企业成功的关键。它超越了交易效率，包括战略寻源、风险管理、合规和财务控制。通过实施数字化转型并利用先进的寻源到付款解决方案，企业不仅能够实现采购目标，还能在不断变化的业务环境中推动可持续的业务增长和竞争优势。许多企业在寻源到付款流程中面临以下难题：

 

  • 手动流程，缺乏自动化
  • 数据质量低下，可视性差
  • 分散的系统和数据孤岛
  • 供应商关系管理不善
  • 复杂的审批流程
  • 独立采购           
  • 有限的支出可视性
  • 无效的供应商授权
  • 发票差异
  • 缺乏战略寻源

 

现在，我们将了解如何利用企业自动化来克服寻源到付款流程中的这些挑战，并确保流程顺畅且高度自动化。但首先，什么是 SAP 的企业自动化？

 

什么是企业自动化？

借助 SAP 的企业自动化功能，我们的客户能够利用 SAP Integration Suite、SAP Signavio 和 SAP Build 等一流的工具，快速、轻松地洞察业务流程，并利用合适的工具实现自动化，实现出色的业务成果。这些工具与现有业务应用程序无缝协作，不会增加复杂性。

  • SAP Integration Suite 是一款集成平台即服务 (iPaaS)，具有数千个与 SAP 和非 SAP 应用程序的预构建集成、API 和连接器。它降低了企业预置和多云环境的复杂性，可帮助您实现优化的端到端业务流程。

 

  • SAP Signavio 提供基于 200,000 个行业基准和最佳实践的业务流程可见性、潜在瓶颈、根本原因分析以及改进建议。深入了解效率低下有助于组织优化和自动化流程。

 

  • SAP Build 是一款低代码解决方案，可以轻松拖放创建自动化和应用。它包括面向 SAP 和非 SAP 系统的预构建自动化和连接器。业务专家可以使用 SAP Build 快速解决流程低效问题。

 

 

寻源到付款的预构建内容：

从 SAP 的企业自动化开始的最简单方法是使用预构建内容。内容提供技术部件，您可以实施这些部件，以及直接来自 SAP 开发团队和合作伙伴的最佳实践和建议。您可以在此处找到我们的内容：

SAP Build – 预构建内容

在这里，您可以找到一些专为优化寻源到付款而设计的内容示例：

创建采购申请 (BAPI)

通过 Excel 创建采购申请

从电子邮件提取供应商发票文件并上载到 SAP Central Invoice Management

创建开票凭证 - VF01 (ECC) Cloud Studio

Ariba 采购申请审批

物料 BOM 更改 - CS02 (ECC)

收货采购订单 - ECC (MIGO)

收货检验批次供应商谈判

采购申请抬头审批和审批操作

创建收货 (BAPI)

使用付款时付款条款的供应商发票的账龄分析

采购申请审批和审批

管理无采购订单的发票

将此内容转化您的优势，因为它不仅为您提供可在 SAP Build Lobby 中部署的技术部件，还为您提供来自开发团队和合作伙伴且经认证可上载 SAP Build 内容的建议和最佳实践。此外，我们还确保定期更新和维护内容。

用例

开始使用企业自动化的最佳方法是使用具体的用例。在这里，我们将重点关注一个场景，该场景经常出现在许多组织中，并且是低效和重复性活动的根本原因：每次需要在 SAP 中处理新的采购订单时，都会包括缺少需要手动添加的主数据。在此，我们正在利用 SAP Signavio Process Intelligence 识别缺少物料代码的采购订单凭证。识别非编码物料后，我们将在 SAP Build 中触发流程以相应地分配物料代码。此外，我们还利用机器学习技术来确定合适的供应商。在此图中，您可以看到非编码物料的自动采购组分配如何：

 

与往常一样，我们建议从 Signavio Process Insights 开始，评估您的流程架构并识别您最大的自动化潜力。在此，您可以深入了解您的寻源到付款领域，并以较低的自动化率查看流程步骤。

您还将收到来自标准 SAP 应用程序或 SAP Build 的直接改进建议和最佳实践。

 

您可以利用 SAP Signavio Process Intelligence 分析成功处理采购订单凭证需要处理的不同步骤。 

在此，我们以复杂的方式评估单个流程实例，并评估采购订单处理的平均周期时间。

 

使用此工具，您可以获得富有洞察力的流程绩效指标，例如缺少物料代码的采购订单数量。

您可以快速意识到，具有非编码物料的采购订单会导致业务用户的中断和重复性任务。

此外，您可以利用与 SAP Build 的本机连接并应用业务逻辑直接触发流程，而无需人工干预。

因此，每次确定非编码采购订单时，我们都会在 SAP Build 中启动流程实例。

为了让人员处于循环中，您可以向业务用户发送审批通知，以进行数据验证和异常处理。

通过此方法，您可以使工作流中的上下文数据可供业务用户查看。

 

使用 SAP Build Process Automation 的低代码设计器执行流程。 

在此场景中，我们启动步骤以识别正确的物料代码，并使用机器学习将正确的供应商映射到每个凭证。在流程逻辑结束时，工作流使用本机连接器直接在 SAP S/4HANA 中过账缺少的物料代码。

 

为了实现非常复杂的集成体系结构，我们还包括 SAP Integration Suite 来处理多个系统中的采购订单凭证。

在此，我们利用预构建的集成流在 SAP Business Suite 企业预置系统中复制采购订单。

 

结论

Enterprise Automation 目前是 SAP 的最热门主题，它允许您在异构环境中集成应用程序、发现低效环节和自动化流程。投资这项计划必将带来长期的业务成果，并采用完善的方法自动运行和管理企业内部的自动化流程。

这只是如何在寻源到付款上下文中使用这些技术的示例。请记住，此框架具有高度适应性，可用于任何业务范围、行业或用例。因此，请务必查看我们的其他资料、赋能和文档，开启 SAP 企业自动化之旅：

 

  • 请访问 sap.com/enterprise-automation
  • 查看 SAP Build Store 中的预构建集成和自动化内容
  • 查看《教程导航器》中的“实践”材料和说明

entry.title: 利用 SAP 的企业自动化处理销售线索到收款流程
entry.content:
本文为翻译文，原文链接Leverage Enterprise Automation with SAP for Lead-to-Cash processes，原文作者Manuel_Namyslo。

简介

在现代业务的动态架构中，企业面临着将潜在销售线索高效转化为收入的挑战。销售线索到现金流程在此过程中起着关键作用，包括从销售线索生成到现金收款的各个阶段。销售、营销和财务职能的无缝集成对于持续的业务增长和成功至关重要。现代销售线索到收款流程包括以下步骤：

 

通过优先考虑从销售线索生成到现金收款的无缝活动流，企业能够优化资源，提升客户体验，并在各自行业中扮演敏捷且响应迅速的企业。但是，许多企业都面临着从销售线索到现金的难题，这与流程管理不佳密切相关：

 

  • 客户旅程不明确
  • 销售线索跟踪和管理流程不足
  • 销售团队与营销团队之间沟通不畅
  • 流程期间的数据捕获不足
  • 数据质量问题
  • 难以准确预测销售目标
  • 客户启用周期较长
  • 延迟开票付款或收款问题

 

在本博文中，我想向您展示如何利用企业自动化来克服销售线索到现金领域的低效流程、重复性任务和不良的流程管理。但首先，什么是 SAP 的企业自动化？

 

什么是企业自动化？

借助 SAP 的企业自动化功能，我们的客户能够利用 SAP Integration Suite、SAP Signavio 和 SAP Build 等一流的工具，快速、轻松地洞察业务流程，并利用合适的工具实现自动化，实现出色的业务成果。这些工具与现有业务应用程序无缝协作，不会增加复杂性：

 

  • SAP Integration Suite 是一款集成平台即服务 (iPaaS)，具有数千个与 SAP 和非 SAP 应用程序的预构建集成、API 和连接器。它降低了企业预置和多云环境的复杂性，可帮助您实现优化的端到端业务流程。
  • SAP Signavio 提供基于 200,000 个行业基准和最佳实践的业务流程可见性、潜在瓶颈、根本原因分析以及改进建议。深入了解效率低下有助于组织优化和自动化流程。
  • SAP Build 是一款低代码解决方案，可以轻松拖放创建自动化和应用。它包括面向 SAP 和非 SAP 系统的预构建自动化和连接器。业务专家可以使用 SAP Build 快速解决流程低效问题。

 

 

 

销售线索到收款的预构建内容

从 SAP 的企业自动化开始的最简单方法是使用预构建内容。内容提供技术部件，您可以实施这些部件，以及直接来自 SAP 开发团队和合作伙伴的最佳实践和建议。您可以在此处找到我们的内容：

SAP Build – 预构建内容

在此，您可以找到一些专为优化从线索到收款而设计的内容示例：

通过 Excel 自动创建销售订单

使用参考创建产品主数据

使用灵活工作流的销售订单审批扩展示例

线索到订单的智能工作流

使用 Excel 通过电子邮件自动创建销售订单

创建客户物料信息记录

批量维护销售合同

根据非结构化数据自动创建销售订单请求

项目经理项目创建

项目活动触发器

 

将此内容用于您的优势 - 因为它不仅为您提供可在 SAP Build Lobby 中部署的技术部件，还为您提供来自开发团队和合作伙伴且经认证可上载 SAP Build 内容的建议和最佳实践。此外，我们还确保定期更新和维护内容。

 

用例

让我们用一个用例来具体了解一下！销售订单处理是“销售线索到现金”领域的常见场景。它可以包括用户体验和流程执行方面的特定需求。例如，数据验证的层次审批步骤或用于分类折扣规则或客户细分的复杂业务规则。或将销售订单创建连接到多个记录系统的特定集成要求。在此，您可以查看场景外观的概览：

我们建议从 SAP Signavio Process Insights 开始。在此，您可以查看流程架构并评估端到端场景，如销售线索到收款、寻源到付款和记录到报表。

根据你的数据发现卓越实践和改进建议，并对标行业标准。在这里，你可以看到“销售线索到收款”业务线中出现的低效场景，这些场景具有较高的自动化潜力。您还可以直接获得 SAP Business Accelerator Hub 中预构建内容的建议：

 

在 SAP Signavio Process Intelligence 中，您可以执行流程挖掘以深入了解整个销售线索到收款流程，并深入了解销售订单凭证创建的每个流程变式。

发现不同的流变化并确定需要改进的瓶颈，例如销售订单凭证的手动处理：

 

 

基于 BPMN 2.0 可视化流程，并查看 SAP S/4HANA 中订单创建的所有必要步骤。您还可以与利益相关方协作，以确定改进领域并确定其优先级。

 

 

发现 SAP Signavio 的流程效率低下后，您可以跳转到 SAP Build 以生成自定义扩展。在这种情况下，我们利用 SAP Build Apps 创建可定制的移动应用，以增强销售订单处理。每个销售代表都可以使用该自定义应用，将新销售订单输入到记录系统中。

为此，我们利用基于低代码的设计时间，并在开发流程中包括多个利益相关方：

 

 

 

还可以使用流程自动化扩展应用程序，以协调工作流场景和 Robotic Process Automation (RPA)。

通过可定制的审批步骤、业务规则、机器学习和与 SAP 和非 SAP 系统的本机连接，在 SAP S/4HANA 中执行销售订单创建流程：

 

 

 

在下一个屏幕截图中，您可以看到用于检查和审批销售订单凭证的嵌入式表单构建器。然后，审批人可以与 SAP Task Center 中的流程任务进行交互，例如批准或拒绝销售订单凭证。

通过此工具，您可以利用直观的拖放功能以及预先开发的 UI 元素：

 

除本机连接器外，您还可以使用表面自动化轻松集成到 SAP 和非 SAP 系统。

这是使用经典 RPA 功能（如屏幕录制和屏幕捕获）自动执行重复和手动任务的常用方法。在这种情况下，我们可以将新的销售订单凭证输入到 SAP S/4HANA 或将交易数据或主数据复制到系统中：

在开发项目中，您会很快注意到的一点是，您无法实现任何无法集成的自动化。尽管 SAP Build 允许您连接到 SAP 和非 SAP 系统，但 SAP Integration Suite 可以将连接引入完全不同的级别。

使用此工具，您可以生成自己的集成流，以将流程连接到异构 IT 架构中的任意类型的应用程序。 利用预构建内容，通过高级值映射和消息转换在第三方应用程序中复制销售订单凭证：

 

 

结论

 

Enterprise Automation 目前是 SAP 的最热门主题，它允许您在异构环境中集成应用程序、发现低效环节和自动化流程。投资这项计划必将带来长期的业务成果，并采用完善的方法自动运行和管理企业内部的自动化流程。

这只是如何在“销售线索到收款”上下文中使用这些技术的示例。请记住，此框架具有高度适应性，可用于任何业务范围、行业或用例。因此，请务必查看我们的其他资料、赋能和文档，开启 SAP 企业自动化之旅：

 

  • 请访问 sap.com/enterprise-automation
  • 查看 SAP Build Store 中的预构建集成和自动化内容
  • 查看《教程导航器》中的“实践”材料和说明

 

当然，如果你想快速开启低代码开发之旅，加入我们的 SAP Builders 社区，开始学习 SAP Build 解决方案！

entry.title: See how SAP Enterprise Support helps customers use SAP Build to empower their digital transformation
entry.content:
SAP Build is a comprehensive suite of low-code and pro-code tools designed to accelerate application development and automation with the power of generative AI. It is built on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) from SAP that helps accelerate development and automation. SAP Build is an enabler for everyone no matter the skill level to create and enhance enterprise applications, process automations, and business sites, all with drag-and-drop simplicity. 

Consisting of three components SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Work Zone, it empowers more users to develop irrespective of their skill level thereby lessening the burden on professional developers.

SAP Build Code is a comprehensive application development environment tailored for developers working with Java and JavaScript. The turnkey solution combines runtime and design time capabilities with built-in enterprise solution integrations, DevOps, and Application Lifecycle Management. Joule, the AI copilot that understands your business, is available to assist with code generation.

In this blog, we are elaborating the assets which are available to provide foundational Support to customers as part of their SAP Enterprise Support cloud edition subscription to use the SAP Build portfolio to empower their digital transformation. 

With SAP Enterprise Support, customers can:

  • Receive guidance and realize value through AI-powered services, best practice content, remote quality checks, and assessment services. 
  • Empower your team to gain knowledge and skills and collaborate with experts for answers to support-related questions.
  • Transform your end-to-end landscape with application lifecycle management solutions to deploy, operate, and optimize cloud and hybrid business solutions.

If you want to just gain an overview of the SAP Build portfolio we have provided this SAP Build Big Picture session. In this e-learning, you will gain an overview of the SAP Build portfolio including SAP Build Code. It discusses the importance of the Clean Core concept and explore the low code/no code tools available for building new applications. Additionally, a high-level overview of SAP Build Code application development, assisted by Joule, is provided. For more detail, we have provided all the overview sessions for the SAP Build portfolio in the learning program Learn to develop using low-code through SAP Build provided through the SAP Enterprise Support Value Map program.

Below we have listed the SAP Build components and the corresponding support provided to customers to get started:

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Process Automation powers the automation of workflows and tasks with comprehensive workflow management, robotic process automation, and AI capabilities.  

To gain an overview to SAP Build Process Automation we have created a Discovery Center mission on Foundational support to start with SAP Build Process Automation. This includes an introductory session which has a demo the tool in action. Also it includes a multi-day Expert Guided Implementation service on SAP Build Process Automation. The purpose of this EGI service is to ramp-up customers to understand and adopt SAP Build Process Automation. In the first step, we give an overview of process automation in the context of SAP Build. Afterwards we show case the capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation and customers learn how to create workflows, decision management, and task automation with powerful yet intuitive no-code capabilities through the configuration of a specific business process to approve Sales Orders automatically. Further information including how to register for the service is provided here.

SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps is an evolution of SAP AppGyver and it enables users to build web and mobile enterprise applications.  With hundreds of templates and pre-built business logic components, you can jumpstart your application projects and reduce development time. 

To gain an overview to SAP Build Process Automation we have created a Discovery Center mission on Foundational support to start with SAP Build Apps. This includes an introductory session. Also it includes a multi-day Expert Guided Implementation service on SAP Build Apps. The purpose of this EGI service is to ramp-up customers to understand and adopt SAP Build Apps. In the first step, we give an overview of application development in the context of SAP Build. Afterwards we show case the capabilities of SAP Build Apps and customers learn how to create mobile and web applications, connect to SAP systems, and deploy and run on SAP Business Technology Platform.​ Further information including how to register for the service is provided here.

SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Work Zone, brings the capabilities of SAP Launchpad and SAP Work Zone into one solution, facilitating the rapid creation of engaging and personalized business sites. It provides centralized access to business application information on any device.

To give an overview on SAP Build Work Zone, we have created the e-learning Explore the power of SAP Build Work Zone - SAP BTP Adoption Tutorial which provides a comprehensive Central Entry Point Digital experience. It encompasses an introduction to essential tools such as SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Task Center, and SAP Mobile Start. Key topics are discussed including the capabilities of SAP Build Work Zone, both standard and advanced editions and Identity and Access management through the SAP Cloud Identity Service. Also, we take a deep dive into the One Inbox suite quality of the SAP Task Center. and SAP Mobile Start as your native mobile entry point.

To provide further information for SAP Build Work Zone, we have created additional e-learnings. Firstly, we give an overview to integrate SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and SAP Mobile Start. We walk you through the configuration process for the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition ensuring you gain a comprehensive understanding of its setup and functionalities. Also, you will gain proficiency in integrating SAP Mobile Start with SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.

Also, we have provided an e-learning for SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition Deep Dive to effectively configure, deploy, and manage SAP Build Work Zone environments, leveraging its capabilities to enhance collaboration and productivity within your organization. The content shows how to set up SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition with integration of IAS (Identity Authentication Service) and IPS (Identity Provisioning Service) for user access, the process of creating a workspace and adding content such as How-to documents and technical support access, developing a UI Card in SAP Business Application Studio and deploying it to the workspace and integrating the Launchpad service and adding exposed content from an SAP S/4HANA system.

SAP Build Code

Apart from the SAP Build Big Picture session which provides an overview to SAP Build Code, we are piloting a new Expert Guided Implementation service on Cloud Application Programming Development using SAP Build Code. The purpose of this service is to empower customers with the knowledge of CAP development in SAP HANA Cloud and provide customers with the knowledge to develop and design in SAP HANA Cloud utilizing the SAP Business Application studio using SAP Build Code and the SAP AI co-pilot Joule. To find out more about this pilot through SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council, please reach out directly.

The e-learning Clean Core with SAP Business Technology Platform offers valuable insights to understand how SAP Business Technology Platform can help cultivate a cleaner and more sustainable core for SAP systems. It offers insights into how SAP BTP can support the fundamental pillars of data, integration, and extensibility. It explores the topic of extensibility in-depth and how the various components of SAP Business Technology Platform support the building of extensions in line with clean core principles.

For customers, if you have issues accessing the above links, there may be an authorizations issue. Please note a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required for most of the above assets. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

For internal colleagues, if you cannot access the above links, please use the following link, click on “Access” and use the above direct link once access has been granted.

We hope that this range of content and assets will support you in your digital transformation. For any queries in relation to any of the above assets, please reach out to us directly.

Thanks and Regards,

Robert Kelly

SAP BTP Lead Architect – SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: SAP Build Apps : MARKETPLACEからダウンロードしたListコンポーネントの任意更新
entry.content:
SAP Build AppsのMARKETPLACEには様々なコンポーネントが存在します。その中でもLIST系のコンポーネントはデータを表示する際に使用するので使用頻度が高いのではと思います。

大体のこれらのコンポーネントにはRefresh Data?とRefresh rateというプロパティが存在し、

Refresh Data?をTrueにすると、Refresh rateのタイミングでリストの内容を再取得し、更新します。

これを"更新ボタンをクリックしたら更新"など任意のタイミングで行えないか？というお問い合わせを頂きましたので、方法を解説したいと思います。

  1. まず、App Variable等でtrue/false型の変数を作成します。ディフォルトはfalseにしておくとよいでしょう。
  2. この変数をリストコンポーネントのRefreshData?プロパティに割り当てます。

     

    これだけで出来るのかと思った方、残念ながらそうではないのです。
  3. ここからが意外と知られていないことなのですが、このリストコンポーネントのロジックに手を加えます。

    このアイコンをクリックしテンプレートエディタを開きます。

    テンプレートエディタではコンポーネントの内部を編集することが可能です。デザインを変えたりも出来ますので結構楽しいですよ。
    テンプレートエディタの下部にあるAdd logic to xxxxxxListをクリックし、ロジック編集に入ります。
  4. これは私愛用のSCROLLABLE ICON LISTのものですが、このようにこのListのロジックが表示されます。結構勉強になるのでどの様に動いているか見ても良いでしょう。

    このロジック、よく見るとComponent mountedイベントをきっかけに動作し、データを取得し、そこでRefresh Data?プロパティがTrueであるならRefresh rate分Delayを行い再度データ取得を行うというロジックです。そうなのです。実はこのロジックではRefresh Data?プロパティの値の変更はTrue→Falseへの変更は反映されますがFalse→Trueへの変更してもデータの再取得は反映されないのです。

  5. Refresh Data?プロパティの値の変更でデータを取得するようにするにはどうしたらよいか？様々な方法が考えられます。私も各人と話し合いましたが簡単なのは

    Receive Eventコントロールを新規に配置し、Event Sourceとして"Internal property 'Refresh Data?' changed"を選択します。これをGet Record Collectionに接続します。

    これにより、Refresh Data?プロパティが変更されたらGet Record Collectionが動作することになります。
  6. 更新ボタン側は簡単です。

    このようにRefresh Data?プロパティに割り当てたtrue/false型変数を、更新したいのであればTrueにしてその後すぐにFalseに戻します。これで"Internal property 'Refresh Data?' changed"イベントが動作し、データがリフレッシュされ、リストが新しい値で更新されます。


    注意：よく見ればわかりますが、上記の変更では実はRefresh Data?プロパティに設定した変数をFalseに変更したときもデータのリフレッシュはかかります。これが気になるのであれば"Internal property 'Refresh Data?' changed"イベントのあとにIF conditionを挟んでTrueのときだけ動作するというロジックに変更して下さい。


このようにすれば任意のタイミングでリストの更新が可能です。Refresh Data?による自動更新では満足できない場合はこの方法をぜひお試し頂ければと思います。

entry.title: Understand CSRF handling in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
What is CSRF?

CSRF stands for Cross-Site Request Forgery. It is a type of malicious attack that tricks the victim into submitting a malicious request. It uses the identity and privileges of the victim to perform an undesired function on their behalf. For instance, this could involve changing an email address or password, or making a purchase. The attack can cause damage even if the exploited action is normally harmless because it's performed without the user's consent or knowledge.

What is the defense mechanism?

CSRF token is a security measure used in web applications to protect against CSRF attacks.

It is a unique, random value associated with a user's session and is typically embedded within a form or URL to ensure that the user is intentionally submitting the request. This token is checked by the server to verify the source of the request, preventing malicious parties from tricking a user's browser into making a request on their behalf.

This is usually taken care of by the browser from where the app is launched and accesses APIs.

CSRF tokens are mainly used for communication with the server. Before an app is allowed to execute a modify operation through an HTTP request such as POST, PUT, DELETE etc., a CSRF token is generated by the server to ensure the security of the operation.

Here is a brief process of how it is used:

  1. First, the app sends a GET request to fetch a CSRF token from the server.
  2. During the GET request, the server generates a new CSRF token and sends it back in the response headers (usually under "x-csrf-token").
  3. The app then uses this token in the header of every modification request.
  4. The server validates the CSRF token in every modifying request. If it's valid, the operation proceeds.
  5. If it's not valid or missing, the server returns a 403 (Forbidden) status, and the operation does not proceed.

In this way, the CSRF token ensures that every operation modifying data is secure, authenticated, and originated from a trusted source. It protects against CSRF attacks where an attacker might trick a victim into executing actions of the attacker's choosing.

Why do we need additional CSRF handling for SAP Build Apps?

To ensure application security, it is essential to understand and manage CSRF token use effectively, as it is crucial in preventing unwanted actions from being performed.

In SAP Build Apps because of the current architecture, extra CSRF token handling is required sometimes for end-user deployed apps with a back-end connection accessing an API indirectly (via a destination such as S/4 HANA) wherein the request to the API goes through via managed app-router.

To be more precise, all installed destinations are CSRF protected by managed app-router, by default. However, if a backend behind a destination has its own CSRF protection, you need to tell SAP Build Apps to switch off managed app-router’s CSRF protection for that specific destination.

This is because when your app is fetching the CSRF token, it will end up getting the CSRF token from managed app-router and then it will send it to backend and fail because the backend doesn’t recognize that token.

What is the consequence of not handling the CSRF in SAP Build Apps?

In this case the symptom would be that the deployed build apps fail with a 403 (forbidden) error as illustrated in one example below.

Disclaimer: As of today it is not possible to show this failure early in the Preview mode as the request handling here (and in the design time) is done via destination-proxy. So in general, no additional CSRF handling is required for design-time and preview mode.

How to overcome this issue?

To overcome this issue, we need to extend your destination (only for some backends which has it's own CSRF protection (such as S/4 Hana backends)) with the tag below to tell the Build Apps to switch off the managed app-router’s CSRF protection for that specific destination.

 

BuildApps.RequiresCsrf = true

 

This way, when your app is fetching the CSRF token, managed app-router won’t interfere and you will get the CSRF token from backend and then when you send it to backend it will work (because the backend will recognize that token).

NOTE: If the SAP Build apps is already built and deployed then we need to also rebuild and redeploy the application for this change in the destination to take effect.

Additional Reading

Refer to the SAP Systems | SAP Help Portal under the 'Adding Data Endpoints' section.

entry.title: The best way to subscribe to SAP Build Apps is through a BTP Booster
entry.content:
Updated: 2 August 2024 - Added more details in case a user does not have a Custom Identity Provider

Updated: 6 August 2024 - Added links to videos showing how to switch from free tier to standard plan, and how to set up SAP Build Apps on a trial account.

 

What is the recommended way to subscribe to SAP Build Apps ?

Most SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) users are familiar with the process of subscribing to BTP services through the BTP Cockpit. However, a subscription to SAP Build Apps is not as easy as it sounds. Besides adding the required entitlements to the tenant, it also requires setting up a Custom IdP (IAS), and much more ! 

For subscribing to SAP Build Apps, please do not use the Service Marketplace, unless you want to configure everything yourself manually and troubleshoot anything you've missed.


BTP Booster

A booster can automate the setup and onboarding of a service. This would obviously include the subscription to the service you want to start using, but also takes care of the prerequisites. A booster can configure entitlements according to service plans available in a specified region, subscribe to various dependent services, configure BTP destinations, and set up the required authentication mechanism.

Where can you find the SAP Build Apps booster ?

Users who have the Global Account Administrator role, will be able to see the booster in the BTP Cockpit of the global account and execute it. If you don't have this role, you'll need to contact the person in your organisation who manages the SAP global account.

To make the search for the correct booster a bit easier, you can use the search function. When you search for Build Apps, you will see two tiles (!) in a productive environment. In a trial account, there will be just one.

For setting up SAP Build Apps, please use the booster called "Get started with SAP Build Apps - Detailed Account Setup".

Before we continue, let me briefly explain why there is another booster. End of last year we've introduced a new way of onboarding through Quick account setup with SAP Discovery Center and Terraform. The booster called "Get started with SAP Build Apps - Quick Account Setup" is part of this initiative. We are currently working on further improvements of the onboarding experience, and will soon replace this booster with an onboarding experience more seamlessly integrated with SAP Discovery Center. Once this goes Live (in the coming months), this second booster will be removed from the booster catalog.

Using the SAP Build Apps booster

Select the booster called "Get started with SAP Build Apps - Detailed Account Setup". This will open the overview page.

In the components tab, you will see which services and subscriptions are handled by the booster. As you can see, subscribing to SAP Build Apps also requires Cloud Foundry Runtime, SAP Mobile Services, Cloud Identity Services and SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.

Let's start the booster by selecting the Start button.

Prerequisites

The first step consists of checking the prerequisites. The booster will be checking whether you have the correct authorizations to execute the booster. It will also check whether the BTP global account has the required entitlements. And last but surely not least, it is checking whether a Custom Identity Provider (IdP) is available on the global account.

If all checks are passed, you can move on to the next step.

In case you have not configured a Custom Identity Provider before, you will see the following 'information button', which you can press for receiving more details.

The 'information button' tells you that the custom identity provider is missing. The booster will automatically add this. During this process, you will receive an email with activation details.

 

Important note for users who unsubscribed from SAP Build Apps and/or switching from free tier to standard plan.

Customers who used Visual Cloud Function (VCF) backends need to wait at least 15 minutes after unsubscribing from SAP Build Apps, before they can subscribe the service again. The current booster will not provide this error information and simply fail.

More details on how to switch the service plan of SAP Build Apps from free tier to standard can be found in this video created by @EsmeeX.

Scenario

Here, you can select the scenario or subaccount mode. You will either create a new subaccount, or apply the booster to an existing subaccount.

Warning: selecting an existing subaccount might impact the way your user(s) authenticate with already subscribed applications in the existing subaccount. A good example here is SAP Business Application Studio. If you have already created dev spaces in SAP Business Application Studio, then these dev space are associated with your default SAP user. Once you switch to authentication through Custom IdP, your user will be different. As such, you will no longer have access to the mentioned dev spaces (until your switch back to the original IdP).

Assuming we create a new subaccount, we select the first option and move on to the next step.

Note: If you have a trial account and have already set up a subaccount, then you will not be able to create another subaccount. In this case, choose the Select Subaccount option.

@Dan_Wroblewski has shared this video showing the steps to set up SAP Build Apps on a trial account.
 

Configuration

In the configuration page you can change the subaccount to your needs and select the service plans you need. The region selection is based on the availability of the service entitlements in the regions. Only regions that offer all listed service entitlements can be selected.

 
After configuring, we move on to the next step.

Adding users

In this step you can add users (email addresses) and select the Identity Provider for platform users as well as application users. For the latter, we will be using the SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS). If your account does not yet have a Custom IdP, it will be created. The required Role Collections will be assigned to the users based on the category Administrators and Developers.

Take note of the information box highlighting:

The Platform Identity Provider displays all the Custom Identity Authentication Service (IAS) tenants configured in your global account. The booster will use it to assign cockpit platform roles like "Subaccount Administrator". Similarly, the Application Identity Provider displays all the Custom IAS tenants associated with the selected subaccount. The IAS tenant will be used by application users having application roles like "BuildAppsDeveloper".

If you don't have a Custom Identity Provider, then the following will be shown: "This will be configured at runtime via Cloud Identity Service".

Review

The last step before executing the booster is to review the selected configurations.

Selecting Finish will start the booster.

Execute booster

All required configuration steps are automated and where possible will run in parallel.

Especially the Establishing Trust, Creating and Assigning Role Collections and mapping them to a user group are troublesome to handle manually.


Result

Upon successful execution of the booster, you will be presented with a link to navigate to the subaccount.

Once in the subaccount's cockpit, you will find the SAP Build Apps service link, which leads you to the SAP Build Lobby, where you can start working with SAP Build Apps. Your user(s) have access to this out-of-the-box.

 

Conclusion

Subscribing to SAP Build Apps requires more than just adding the service entitlement and subscribing to it. Please use the BTP Booster for SAP Build Apps which automates all the steps for you.

Happy "no-coding" with SAP Build Apps !

entry.title: How to add a search bar with suggestions in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hello community,

In this blog, I’ll show you how to add a search bar with suggestions to your SAP Build Apps application.

Scenario Overview

Imagine you have an app where you need to search through a list of manufacturers containing over 40,000 entities. Efficiently navigating such a large dataset can be challenging. You want to search using a search bar while also being able to manually browse the list.

Step 1: Creating the Search Component and a Data Variable for the Manufacturers List

Start by creating a component that serves as your search bar, along with a button to open the full list of manufacturers. Here’s how to do it:

  1. Input Field & Icon: Add a simple input field for the search. Next, add an icon (I used journey-arrive from the Fiori icon set) and position it on the right side of the input field. This icon will function as a button to open the complete list of manufacturers.
  2. Data Variable: Create a data variable linked to the data source to retrieve the list of manufacturers.
  3. Text Element: Add a text element that repeats for each manufacturer in your list, stored in a data variable. This list is visible only if your search variable is not empty and the current repeated element does not match the search variable.
    Visibility Formula:
!IS_EMPTY(appVars.selected_manufacturer) && appVars.selected_manufacturer != repeated.current.manufacturerName

Note: Here, selected_manufacturer is stored as an app variable because it's needed outside of this page. However, if it's only required on this page, you can use a page variable instead.

Step 2: Displaying the Manufacturer List

When you click on the search bar icon, it should navigate to a page displaying your manufacturer list. This list is fetched using pagination. If you need help with pagination, refer to this blog on handling OData pagination in SAP AppGyver.

Step 3: Implementing Search Functionality

The search process should be triggered when the variable tied to your custom component changes. You can use the following approach when retrieving your record collection from the backend:

  1. Get record collection: Search with the formula, when a user enters a search term in the search bar, you want to filter the list of manufacturers based on this term. This is achieved through a formula:
{"type":"and","conditions":[{"type":"containsIgnoreCase","property":["manufacturerName"],"value":appVars.selected_manufacturer }]}
  •   type: The filtering method. "containsIgnoreCase" means the search will be case-insensitive and will look for the term anywhere within the property’s value.
  •   property: The field in your data to search within. "manufacturerName" should match the field name in your data variable where manufacturer names are stored.
  •   value: This is the search term, taken from appVars.selected_manufacturer, which holds the user’s input from the search bar.

2. Set Data Variable: Slicing the results. After applying the search filter, limit the results to the first eight records:

SLICE(outputs["Get record collection"].records, 0,8)

Alternative Approaches

Here are some alternative methods and tools that can be used for implementing a search bar with suggestions in SAP Build Apps:

OData Search Capability

  • Description: If your OData service supports a built-in search capability, you can leverage this feature directly without the need for complex filtering logic. For more details, refer to this blog: How to Filter Data in SAP Build Apps Using Record Collection.
  • Advantages: This method simplifies the implementation process since the search is handled by the OData service itself.
  • Limitations: Ensure that your OData service supports search operations. 

Search Bar with Suggestions (Video Tutorial)

  • Description: This video tutorial provides a step-by-step guide on creating a search bar with suggestions in SAP Build Apps: Search bar with suggestions || SAP Build Apps.
  • Advantages: It’s a practical guide for scenarios with a small amount of records.
  • Limitations: This approach may not be suitable for large datasets as it only searches among the first few retrieved records.

Combo Box Component

  • Description: The SAP Build Apps marketplace offers a ready-to-use Combo Box component that provides a dropdown with search capabilities.
  • Advantages: It's a straightforward solution that doesn’t require additional pages or custom components.
  • Limitations: This component might not be ideal if you need to search through very large datasets, as you would need to scroll through a drop-down.

Conclusion

Depending on your specific requirements, you can choose from several approaches to implement a search bar with suggestions in SAP Build Apps. For smaller datasets, you can refer to the video tutorial included in this blog. Alternatively, you can use a pre-built component for simpler implementations or build a custom component to handle larger datasets.

Now, go ahead and build something amazing!

entry.title: Changes to SAP Build Apps UI Canvas
entry.content:
There are 3 key changes to the UI canvas in SAP Build Apps that I have noticed in the last few days. Let me know if you see others, and let me know what you think about the changes.

Change Container's Horizontal / Vertical Layout in the UI Canvas

It used to be that you had to go all the way into a container's Layout tab, then open the Layout area, and then change from horizontal (the default) to vertical.

Now you can do this directly in the UI canvas with 1-click.

This sounds like a small thing, but his was a task done ALL THE TIME for a container, and in any sophisticated UI it was likely you would have dozens of containers. This and the ability to update texts directly in the UI canvas save a lot of time.

 

Component's Core Tab Shuffled

This will actually cost a little time at the beginning but eventually will save ime. The components in the Core tab have been shuffled to make it easier to find the components used most often.

Based on old screenshots, this is what I was able to piece together:

  • Container has been elevated to the very first component.
  • The 1st grouping is now called "Essentials" instead of "Display". Title and Text remain, but are joined with the very common UI elements Button, Input Field and Dropdown Field. (The Card component has been removed completely.)
  • The 2nd grouping is now called "Inputs" instead of "Forms". It includes the remaining components (Slider, Checkbox, Password) along with the elevated Search component.
  • The 3rd group is now called "Display" instead of "Layout". The Scroll View and Row components are now joined by the 3 demoted components from the old Display area: Image, Icon and Spinner. 
  • The 4th and last group, "Lists" is pretty much as it was. All the same components appear, except List Divider has been removed, and the Search Bar has been moved higher up. 

 

Easier to Find Logic You've Written

A common complaint I've heard is that it is difficult to find all the logic you've written. For example, if you are setting the value of a variable and want to change the behavior, it can be hard to find where you added a "Set page variable" flow function.

In a start to help developers, there is now an indicator that tells you whether you have logic associated with a component.

A green dot appears on the UI canvas when you highlight that component, and the same green dot appears next to that component in the tree view.

NOTE: If your logic canvas only has Receive Event flow functions, this is not considered as logic. If you drag other flow functions onto the logic canvas, this is considered having logic even if they are not connected to any event. Also, I do not see any indicator about logic in the main canvas logic area, nor in the variable canvas area.

Leave a comment about what you think about the changes.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 1 - Setup
entry.content:
SAP AI Core is a service in the SAP Business Technology Platform that is designed to handle the execution and operations of your AI assets in a standardized, scalable, and hyperscaler-agnostic way. It provides seamless integration with your SAP solutions. Any AI function can be easily realized using open-source frameworks. SAP AI Core supports full lifecycle management of AI scenarios. Access generative AI capabilities and prompt lifecycle management via the generative AI hub.

SAP Build Apps is a professional application development solution designed for anyone to quickly create apps without code regardless of role or skill level.

In this series of blogs, I will showcase how to integrate SAP AI Core services into SAP Build Apps to develop no-code applications with AI capabilities.

1. Subscribe SAP AI Core service and create instance

refer to https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-launchpad-provisioning.html and https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-generative-ai.html for details on how to setup SAP AI Core service and SAP AI Launchpad.

 

2. Note down the "client-id" and "client-secret" under credentials of your new instance

 

3. Create new deployment in SAP AI Launchpad and note down the url

4. Configure new destination for SAP Build Apps to consume the new AI service deployment

an example of destination configuration is as below

#
#Wed Jul 03 03:57:27 UTC 2024
Type=HTTP
clientId=sb-xxxxxxxxx|xsuaa_std\!xxxxx
clientSecret=xxxxxxxxxxxx
HTML5.DynamicDestination=true
Authentication=OAuth2ClientCredentials
URL.headers.AI-Resource-Group=default
Name=AzureOpenAI
tokenServiceURL=https\://test-int.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
URL=https\://api.ai.prod.eu-central-1.aws.ml.hana.ondemand.com/v2/inference/deployments/d123456789c
ProxyType=Internet
tokenServiceURLType=Dedicated
URL.headers.Content-Type=application/json
AppgyverEnabled=true
sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled=true
WebIDEEnabled=true

 

 

After completing the steps mentioned above, our next blog will explore integrating SAP AI Core services into SAP Build Apps to develop no-code applications with AI capabilities.

Link to next blog: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 2 - Integration via Javascript function

entry.title: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 2 - Integration via Javascript function
entry.content:
If you want maximum flexibility and complete control over your logic flow and don't mind writing your own code, creating a JavaScript function can be an option for integrating SAP AI Core service into your SAP Build Apps application.

1. Configure the BTP destination REST API integration in SAP Build Apps. Unlike integration with the REST API flow function, you don't need to configure additional common request headers and resource schema, as these will be specified in a JavaScript function.

 

 

2. Use the JavaScript flow function to integrate with the new REST API. Note that this integration currently only functions in the deployed app due to the limitations of the destination proxy of SAP Build Apps; it does not work in preview mode. As demonstrated in the sample code below, you will need to manage the CSRF token by first fetching it, saving it, and then using it for POST requests.

sample code of Javascript function

 

let user_input = inputs.user_input;
let system_input = inputs.system_input;
console.log(user_input, system_input);

let text = ''; // Initialize text variable to store the response text

try {
  const svc_url = "destinations/AzureOpenAI/chat/completions?api-version=2024-02-01";

  // Fetch CSRF token from the server
  const csrfResponse = await fetch(svc_url, { method: "GET", headers: { "X-CSRF-Token": "Fetch" } });
  const csrf_token = csrfResponse.headers.get('x-csrf-token'); // Use .get() to retrieve header value

  // Data object for POST request
  const data = {
    messages: [
      { role: "system", content: system_input },
      { role: "user", content: user_input }
    ],
    max_tokens: 1000,
    temperature: 0.5,
    frequency_penalty: 0,
    presence_penalty: 0,
    stop: null // Use null without quotes for actual null value
  };

  // Headers including CSRF token
  const headers = {
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
    "ai-resource-group": "default",
    "X-Csrf-Token": csrf_token
  };

  // Fetch request to the service URL
  const fetchResponse = await fetch(svc_url, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: headers,
    body: JSON.stringify(data)
  });

  // Check if the response is ok (status in the range 200-299)
  if (!fetchResponse.ok) {
    throw new Error(`HTTP error! status: ${fetchResponse.status}`);
  }

  // Parse JSON response
  const responseData = await fetchResponse.json();
  console.log(responseData);
  // Assign the response text to the variable
  text = responseData.choices[0].message.content;

} catch (error) {
  // Log any errors that occur during the fetch operation
  console.error(error);
}

// Return the text
  const new_obj = { text };
  console.log(new_obj);
  return new_obj;

 

3. Design your page and UI controls in SAP Build Apps, then open the logic canvas to add logic to the desired UI control, include the JavaScript function in the logic, and add input and output parameters to the JavaScript function. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

4. Deploy your app and run it

Link to previous blog: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 1 - Setup 

Link to next blog: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 3 - Integration via REST API

entry.title: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 3 - Integration via REST API
entry.content:
Another option for integrating SAP AI Core service into your SAP Build Apps application is to use the existing core flow function "Create Record" via BTP destination REST API integration.

1. Configure the BTP destination REST API Integration in SAP Build Apps. You need to configure the additional common request headers and resource schema.

 

2. Design your page and UI controls in SAP Build Apps, then open the logic canvas to add logic to the desired UI control, include the "Create Record" flow function in the logic, and configure the input and output parameters accordingly.

 

3. Use the Create Record flow function to consume the new REST API Integration. Please note that although we use the "Create Record flow function," we are not actually creating any new records in the backend AI service. Instead, we use this flow function to send a "Post" request, allowing the AI service to receive the "prompt" and respond accordingly. After receiving the response from the AI service, we need to further process the response, e.g., parse it and assign variables.

 

 

4. Deploy your app and run it

Link to previous blog: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 2 - Integration via Javascript function

entry.title: Get Started with SAP BTP Document Management Service (video)
entry.content:
Many of you may have been introduced to the SAP BTP Document Management Service in using SAP Build process Automation, which has some integrations with DMS so your process can allow users to upload documents and you can add those documents to approval forms.

DMS also provides other capabilities, such as versioning, search, and access control, and can be used as a content server from the SAP S/4HANA attachment service. @AlexDong has written a wonderful blog collecting a lot of the information about DMS, especially from an SAP S/4HANA perspective.

To help, especially for newbies who've seen DMS from SAP Build perspective, I have created a TechByte video to show what DMS is, the different parts of DMS, how to install the Integration (API) Option, and how to make API calls to work with repos and documents.

In future videos, we'll look at the application option for doing the same thing but with a GUI, how to access documents on mobile, and finally how to work with DMS in SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps.

entry.title: Partner Demo Environment - Phase 10 AI Feature Release
entry.content:
We are excited to share with you the latest updates to our PDE (Shared Demo Environment) Phase 10 AI Release.

This release introduces a range of cutting-edge AI features across various SAP solutions, designed to enhance productivity, streamline processes, and drive better business outcomes. 

In this release, Gen-AI and Joule features are available across the suite, including Customer Experience (2), #Ariba(2), #SuccessFactors#(1), and BTP(1)

Sales & Service Cloud V2

SAP Sales & Service Cloud Version 2 now features cutting-edge Generative AI capabilities designed to enhance sales and service processes. These innovative features leverage AI to streamline workflows, improve decision-making, and enhance customer interactions.

The following table provides an overview of the key Generative AI features available in this solution:

FeatureDescription and FunctionalityKey Business Benefits
Case SummaryGenerative AI summarizes text in email interactions for cases, providing a quick overview of customer communications.- Improved efficiency in case handling- Faster response times due to quick comprehension of case details- Enhanced customer service quality through better understanding of issues
Email DrafterUses Generative AI to draft email replies or interactions for cases and opportunities, streamlining communication processes.- Time savings in composing emails- Consistency in messaging across the organization- Improved quality and professionalism of communications
Account SynopsisProvides comprehensive information related to an account, helping sales and service professionals. It covers:
• Overall Information
• Business Overview
• Current Challenges
• Competitive Landscape
• Culture and Background
• Business Strategies
• Typical Customers		- Holistic view of customer accounts- Better prepared sales and service teams- More informed decision-making in customer interactions
Lead BoosterOffers customized information about a lead to help sales representatives win deals. It includes:
• About the Company
• Challenges
• Alignment with Customer Goals
• Customer Pain Points
• Benefits
• Customization and Scalability
• Competitive Advantage Maximization		- Helps sales representatives win the deal (stated in document)- Accelerates lead-to-opportunity conversion- Enables more personalized and effective sales strategies

Demo Asset 19197

Customer Experience 

SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement

Leverages advanced AI capabilities to optimize various aspects of digital marketing. The following features enhance marketer efficiency and campaign effectiveness through AI-powered tools.

FeatureDescription and FunctionalityKey Business Benefits
AI Subject Line Generator- Generates or improves subject lines with AI-driven suggestions- Includes personalization and emojis- Enables A/B testing of AI-generated and traditional subject lines- Saves time and effort in creating subject lines- Enhances creativity with new subject-line ideas- Enables data-driven decisions through A/B testing
AI Content Block Generator- Creates personalized content blocks based on natural language prompts- Optimizes content while ensuring brand tone of voice- Assists in product search for campaign insertion- Creates personalized content across customer engagement channels- Accelerates campaign content creation- Maintains brand consistency- Drives greater value from product catalog data- Improves content relevance and personalization
AI Product Finder (Pilot)- Enables intuitive search of a brand's product catalog- Quickly locates suitable products for campaigns- Out-of-the-box solution for email marketing campaigns- Improves efficiency in product selection for campaigns- Scales creation of curated product content- Enhances message relevancy and engagement
AI Segment Generator (Pilot)- Generates human-readable descriptions of existing segments- Can create new segments from natural language prompts- Distills complex criteria, logic, and operators into comprehensible descriptions- Improves understanding of customer segments- Enhances searchability of segments- Simplifies creation of new, targeted segments
  • The Subject Line Generator is set for General Availability in July 2024.
  • The AI Product Finder and AI Segment Generator are currently in the pilot phase.
  • These features are part of SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement, integrated with SAP Business AI.
  • The demo scenario uses specific email templates and segments. It's recommended not to alter these for demonstration purposes.

Demo Asset 19326

SAP Emarsys Loyalty 

 

SAP Business Technology Platform

This summary highlights the AI features provided by SAP Build Code for enhancing customer loyalty programs and application development. These features leverage generative AI to streamline the development process and improve customer engagement.

 

Feature NameDescription and FunctionalityKey Business Benefits
AI-powered Code Generation with Joule Copilot- Generates app logic from natural language descriptions- Optimized for Java and JavaScript application development- Provides a turn-key environment for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management- Increases developer productivity- Enables rapid application development- Simplifies complex coding tasks- Reduces time-to-market for new applications
Data Entity Generation- Creates data models and service entities using natural language prompts- Automatically generates sample data for testing and previewing- Accelerates database design process- Ensures consistency in data structure- Reduces errors in data modeling
Backend Logic Generation- Generates backend logic for operations like purchases, reward points calculation, and redemptions- Creates complex business rules from simple natural language descriptions- Streamlines implementation of business logic- Ensures consistency in applying business rules. Reduces development time for core functionalities
Fiori UI Application Design- Generates user interfaces for entities using AI-assisted templates- Creates responsive applications suitable for various devices

- Accelerates UI development process- Ensures consistency in user experience across applications

- Improves overall application usability and adoption

Fusion Development Approach- Combines professional developers' work (using Build Code) with citizen developers' contributions (using Build Apps)- Enables seamless integration between backend services and frontend applications- Facilitates collaboration between IT and business users- Speeds up overall application development lifecycle- -Allows for more flexible and agile development processes
  • The solution is based on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and benefits from its security features for authentication, authorization, and data protection.
  • SAP Build Code is optimized for SAP development and provides prebuilt integrations, APIs, and business services.
  • The solution enables quick extension of enterprise applications such as SAP S/4HANA.
  • Sample data generated by the AI is intended for testing and demo purposes only and should not be deployed to production.
  • The AI-generated code may vary for the same prompts, but as long as it achieves the desired functionality, it can be used.

Demo Asset 19247

SAP Build Code Generation Interactive Value Journey

SAP Build Code Joule Artificial Intelligence 

 

SAP Ariba Category Management

SAP Ariba Category Management offers a comprehensive suite of tools to enhance category planning, strategy execution, and procurement processes.

This summary covers both AI-powered features and core functionalities described in the attached documents.

Feature NameDescription and FunctionalityKey Business Benefits
Generative AI-Enhanced Content Recommendations- Available for Category Segmentation, Market Dynamics, and Cost Structure tools- Prefills content in Notes, Summary, and Additional Resources sections based on AI-retrieved content- Provides additional information articles based on category, organization, region, and other input parameters- Accelerates category strategy development- Provides contextual insights for better decision-making- Reduces time spent on manual research and data gathering- Enhances the quality and depth of category analysis
BEROE Integration- Integrates market intelligence reports from BEROE into category planning- Provides news feed in Strategy Execution dashboard with BEROE data- Allows users to add data from BEROE provider in Market Dynamics tool- Enhances market analysis with external expert insights- Keeps category managers informed of market trends and news- Improves strategic decision-making with up-to-date market intelligence- Reduces time spent on manual market research
Category Segmentation- Uses Kraljic matrix to segment categories based on business impact and supply market complexity- Visualizes subcategories within the main category- Supports AI-generated content for segmentation analysis- Helps in strategic categorization of spend- Offers insights for tailored category strategies- Facilitates better risk and opportunity management
Market Dynamics Analysis- Utilizes Porter's Five Forces model- Integrates with BEROE for market intelligence data- Supports AI-generated content- Provides comprehensive view of market forces- Enhances understanding of competitive landscape- Aids in developing more informed sourcing strategies
Cost Structure Analysis- Breaks down category costs into components- Employs AI to generate cost structure content- Offers insights into cost drivers- Improves understanding of cost drivers- Aids in identifying cost reduction opportunities- Facilitates more effective negotiations with suppliers
Strategy and Implementation Planning- Allows setting of category goals, value levers, and initiatives- Links initiatives to specific goals- Estimates benefits and investments for initiatives- Aligns category strategy with organizational objectives- Provides clear roadmap for strategy execution- Facilitates tracking of strategy implementation and results
Integration with SAP Ariba Sourcing- Links category initiatives to Guided Sourcing projects- Allows tracking of sourcing project progress within Category Management- Ensures seamless execution of category strategies- Provides visibility into sourcing activities- Facilitates measurement of strategy effectiveness
Monitor Strategy Execution- Displays progress on category goals- Shows status of ongoing initiatives- Integrates with planning calendar, contracts, and sourcing events- Enables real-time tracking of strategy execution- Facilitates proactive management of category performance- Provides holistic view of category-related activities
Category Profile Management- Stores category-specific information (laws, regulations, team members, stakeholders)- Supports document uploads for relevant policies and regulations- Centralizes critical category information- Ensures compliance with relevant laws and policies- Facilitates stakeholder management
  • Category taxonomy management is an administrative function, demonstrated separately through an InVision demo link.
  • The solution integrates with SAP Analytics Cloud for spend analysis visualizations.

Demo Asset 19218

Demo Asset 19219

Category Management Interactive Value Journey

SAP Ariba solution integration for SAP Business Suite 

 

SAP SuccessFactors 

SAP SuccessFactors has integrated advanced AI features to enhance the recruitment process and overall employee experience. These features leverage artificial intelligence to streamline HR operations, improve job description creation, and facilitate better feedback processes.

The following summary highlights the key AI feature: AI-enhanced job descriptions.

AI-Enhanced Job Descriptions• Uses generative AI to create compelling job descriptions
• Leverages data from job requisitions and existing job profiles
